label: Fine china & porcelain (SCAFFOLD — thin sources, uncalibrated; provisional/flagged only until Tier-1 + calibration land)

captureSlots.label: Backstamp macro
captureSlots.guidance: Macro of the backstamp / factory mark on the base.

captureSlots.label: Base and foot rim
captureSlots.guidance: Full base: foot rim wear, paste colour, incised or impressed marks.

captureSlots.label: Rim / gilding detail
captureSlots.guidance: Rim and gilding under raking light — wear, restoration, overpaint.

captureSlots.label: Set layout
captureSlots.guidance: Full service laid out, if a set.

redFlags.label: Spurious mark
redFlags.description: Later or counterfeit backstamp imitating a premium manufactory.

redFlags.label: Later decoration
redFlags.description: Genuine blank decorated later outside the factory and sold as factory-decorated.

redFlags.label: Concealed restoration
redFlags.description: Overpainted rim chips, sprayed glaze, or rebuilt elements.

redFlags.label: Pattern / mark mismatch
redFlags.description: Pattern or form inconsistent with the period the mark claims.

redFlags.label: Reproduction body
redFlags.description: Modern paste and glaze inconsistent with the claimed manufactory and period.

corpusSources.name: Manufactory archives and pattern books (Meissen, KPM, Royal Copenhagen)
